video:video.video:title: Some Palestinians see little hope for reconstruction in Gaza amid intensifying war in Middle East
video:video.video:description: The United Nations estimates the entire enclave has more than 61 million tons of debris. Meanwhile Palestinians continue to live in tents amid the rubble, without access to infrastructure or facilities.

video:video.video:title: 23 dead, flights disrupted and military deployed after heavy rains pound Kenya's capital overnight
video:video.video:description: Twenty-three people died, flights were disrupted and the military was deployed as motorists in the Kenyan capital were stranded for hours on Saturday after roads were flooded following heavy rains overnight.

video:video.video:title: Chinese company showcases largest electrical aircraft, but analyst says flying taxis are years off
video:video.video:description: A Chinese company has developed what it claims is the world’s largest flying electrical vertical take-off and landing vehicle, or eVTOL.

video:video.video:title: UK's chief of defence staff on deployments to the Middle East and UK's preparedness
video:video.video:description: The British military deployed four additional Royal Air Force (RAF) Typhoon fighter jets to Qatar on Saturday as the conflict escalates.

video:video.video:title: Explosions light up the sky following airstrikes on western Tehran
video:video.video:description: Several explosions lit up the sky in western Tehran as Israeli warplanes pounded the Iranian capital in the early hours of Saturday.

video:video.video:title: Iranian President rejects US demand for an unconditional surrender
video:video.video:description: Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian also apologized on Saturday for Iran's attacks on neighbouring countries, suggesting it was the result of miscommunication in the ranks and insisted that it would stop from now on.

video:video.video:title: Travel insiders give advice amidst Iran war travel chaos
video:video.video:description: The Iran war continues to upended travel across the Middle East and beyond. After almost a week of conflict, airspace closures and flight cancelations, travel industry insiders recommend postponing unnecessary travel if possible, checking refund or insurance policies and, most importantly, monitorin

video:video.video:title: After a president-filled celebration, Rev. Jesse Jackson's family gathers for an intimate homegoing
video:video.video:description: A day after former presidents, sitting governors and local Chicago residents alike attended a vibrant, televised celebration for the late Rev.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories March 7
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Saturday, March 7th: Trump encourages Latin American leaders to use military action against cartels; Iranian President apologizes to Gulf Arab states: Oil and gas prices rapidly rise as Iran war continues; Severe weather kill at least six in the U.S.

video:video.video:title: Residents of Michigan community come together in wake of deadly storm
video:video.video:description: Residents of a Michigan community are coming together to pick up the pieces after a deadly storm passed through. (AP video: Mike Householder)

video:video.video:title: Indian expat in UAE opens his farmhouse to hundreds of tourists stranded by flight disruption
video:video.video:description: Three huge tents were set up on the farm to accommodate travellers whose flights had been cancelled due to the ongoing war between the US-Israel and Iran.

video:video.video:title: Smoke seen rising from warehouses near Irbil airport in Iraq's northern Kurdish region
video:video.video:description: Smoke from a suspected drone attack was seen rising from warehouses belonging to various international oil and construction companies near Irbil International Airport in Iraq on Saturday.

video:video.video:title: Renaissance master Michelangelo as revered as ever centuries on
video:video.video:description: The Italian sculptor and painter Michelangelo's genius and legacy are as strong as ever 500 years after his death.

video:video.video:title: 'This had to be done': Trump claims to have done the world a 'favor' with attacks on Iran
video:video.video:description: It comes as Iran's president apologized on Saturday for attacks on “neighboring countries” even as its missiles and drones flew toward Gulf Arab states and Trump threatened that the country would be “hit very hard.”

video:video.video:title: Chappell Roan, Paris Jackson attend Vivienne Westwood show
video:video.video:description: Andreas Kronthaler presented his latest collection for Vivienne Westwood in Paris, inspired by the works of Italian costume designer Danilo Donati and actress Romy Schneider.

video:video.video:title: More airstrikes hit Tehran
video:video.video:description: More airstrikes hit Iran's capital of Tehran as night falls on Saturday.

video:video.video:title: Explosions and flames seen in southern Tehran following new airstrikes
video:video.video:description: Several airstrikes hit neighborhoods in Tehran’s east and southwest Saturday night as Israel’s military confirmed a new wave of attacks. They came after U.S.

video:video.video:title: Ukrainian soldiers commemorate commander Dmytro "Da Vinci" Kotsiubailo during oath-taking ceremony
video:video.video:description: Dmytro Kotsiubailo, callsign Da Vinci, was killed in action three years ago. Soldiers gathered on the grave of Kotsiubailo in Kyiv, singing the national anthem during an oath-taking ceremony for new recruits.

video:video.video:title: Nightclub bombing in Peru injures 33, including minors, authorities say
video:video.video:description: A bombing at a nightclub in Peru has injured 33 people, including minors, authorities said Saturday.

video:video.video:title: Jan. 6 plaque honoring police officers is installed at the Capitol after a 3-year delay
video:video.video:description: Visitors to the Capitol in Washington now have a visible reminder of the violent attack against the building on Jan. 6, 2021, and the officers who fought and were injured that day.

video:video.video:title: Lebanese village wakes up to destruction after 16 killed in night of Israeli airstrikes
video:video.video:description: Residents of a town in Lebanon’s eastern Bekaa Valley walked among the rubble and looked in disbelief Saturday at the vast destruction from overnight clashes that left 16 dead and 35 wounded, according to Lebanon's Health Ministry.

video:video.video:title: At least 7 killed in Ukraine's Kharkiv after Russian missile hits apartment building
video:video.video:description: Russia has fired tens of thousands of Iranian-designed Shahed drones at Ukraine since it invaded its neighbor just over four years ago.

video:video.video:title: Trump joins grieving families for the return of soldiers killed in war in the Middle East
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump on Saturday joined grieving families at Dover Air Force Base at the dignified transfer for the six U.S. soldiers killed in the war in the Middle East.

video:video.video:title: Trump encourages Latin American leaders to use military action to help US fight cartels
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump said Saturday that the U.S. and Latin American countries were banding together to combat violent cartels.

video:video.video:title: Iranian president on demand of unconditional surrender: 'They should take that dream to their grave'
video:video.video:description: Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian apologized on Saturday for Iran's attacks on neighboring countries, suggesting it was the result of miscommunication in the ranks and insisted that it would stop from now on.

video:video.video:title: Trump downplays reports that Russia is providing Iran information on US targets in the Middle East
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump said it's inconsequential if Russia has provided Iran with information to help Tehran target U.S. military personnel and assets in the Middle East.

video:video.video:title: Israel claims to have struck Iran airport it says was used to support militants in Middle East
video:video.video:description: The Israeli military on Saturday reported that it had struck Mehrabad Airport in Tehran that it alleged was used to transport weapons to militant groups that Iran supports across the Middle East.

video:video.video:title: Black smoke billows into Beirut sky after explosion as Israel continues to launch strikes
video:video.video:description: Israel renewed its assault on southern Lebanon early Sunday, including targeting commanders of the Lebanese branch of Iran’s Revolutionary Guards’ Quds Force, after Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu promised “many surprises” for the next phase of the conflict.

video:video.video:title: Top Iranian security official insists 'no divisions' in Tehran leadership over war
video:video.video:description: Top Iranian security official Ali Larijani has asserted that "our leaders are united... and have no disagreements with one another".

video:video.video:title: Israeli drone strike hits hotel in central Beirut - AP Explains
video:video.video:description: Israel says it targeted Iranian-linked commanders in Beirut after a strike hit the Ramada Hotel, popular with tourists and business travelers, and was heard by residents nearby.

video:video.video:title: Black smoke engulfs parts of Iran's capital Tehran after strikes that hit oil depot and refinery
video:video.video:description: A cloud of black smoke covered parts of Tehran on Sunday morning following the overnight strikes that hit an oil depot and refinery.

video:video.video:title: Hometown Halfpipe Hero: Eileen Gu leads San Francisco’s Chinese New Year Parade
video:video.video:description: Olympic freestyle skiing champion Eileen Gu returned to her hometown of San Francisco as Grand Marshal of the San Francisco Chinese New Year Parade, receiving a warm welcome from crowds despite past controversy over her decision to compete for China in the Olympics instead of the United States.

video:video.video:title: Trump says US 'looking forward to the great change that will soon be coming to Cuba'
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump said Saturday that the U.S. was "looking forward to the great change that will soon be coming to Cuba," and suggested that the administration will cut a deal with Havana.

video:video.video:title: Fires send huge clouds of smoke into Tehran night sky after barrage of Israeli airstrikes
video:video.video:description: Pillars of flame rose above an oil storage facility in Tehran late on Saturday as Israel carried out further air strikes.

video:video.video:title: Iran’s president threatens more attacks on US targets
video:video.video:description: Iran’s president has threatened to step up attacks on American targets throughout the Middle East as the U.S. and Israel press ahead with their air campaign.

video:video.video:title: Smoke covers parts of Tehran after Israel strikes Iran oil depot and refinery
video:video.video:description: A cloud of black smoke covered parts of Tehran on Sunday morning following the overnight strikes that hit an oil depot and refinery.

video:video.video:title: Motorists queue for petrol in Bangladesh, amid fears Iran war could spark serious fuel shortage
video:video.video:description: Many even waited overnight in many fuel stations in Dhaka, the capital, to get fuel under a government rationing system allowing cars and bikes to collect a limited amount of fuel.

video:video.video:title: Family allege covert Israeli abduction after retired Lebanese security officer vanishes
video:video.video:description: Retired Lebanese security officer Captain Ahmed Shukr vanished in December after going to meet a would-be buyer of a plot of land he was trying to sell.

video:video.video:title: Iran’s president threatens more attacks on US targets
video:video.video:description: Iran’s president has threatened to step up attacks on American targets throughout the Middle East as the U.S. and Israel press ahead with their air campaign.

video:video.video:title: Kansas City airport evacuated as authorities, including the FBI, investigate a potential threat
video:video.video:description: Portions of Kansas City International Airport were being evacuated Sunday afternoon as authorities looked into a potential threat, the Kansas City Aviation Department said.

video:video.video:title: Israeli drone strike hits hotel in central Beirut
video:video.video:description: Israel says it targeted Iranian-linked commanders in an attack in Lebanon’s capital. An Israeli drone struck the hotel in Beirut’s Raouche district, a major seaside tourist area, killing four people and wounding 10 others, according to Lebanon’s health ministry.

video:video.video:title: Iranian state TV says son of late supreme leader has been named his successor
video:video.video:description: Iranian state TV on Sunday announced that Mojtaba Khamenei, the son of the late supreme leader, had been named his successor.

video:video.video:title: Oil refinery in Tehran blanketed by black smoke on day after it was hit by strikes
video:video.video:description: Iran says that overnight strikes hit four oil storage tankers and a petroleum transfer terminal, killing four people.

video:video.video:title: Cyprus audits its underground shelters after Iranian drone strike on British airbase
video:video.video:description: Cypriot authorities are working to get long-neglected underground shelters back in shape after Iranian drones struck a British airbase on the Mediterranean island on Monday.

video:video.video:title: On International Women’s Day, women in Gaza say they are suffering with no end in sight
video:video.video:description: Palestinian women in Gaza reminisced about their livelihood before the war. The majority of them live starkly different lives in tents that do little to protect them from winter rains and the scorching summer heat, and spend hours every day in pursuit of basic necessities.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories March 8
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Sunday, March 8: Israel strikes oil refinery in Tehran; Police in Norway investigate explosion outside US Embassy; Women around the globe mark International Women’s Day; Barking dogs take over downtown Anchorage for Iditarod start.

video:video.video:title: Iranians celebrate after state TV announces Mojtaba Khamenei named as supreme leader
video:video.video:description: Mojtaba Khamenei, the son of Iran's late supreme leader, has been named his successor, Iranian state TV announced early Monday.

video:video.video:title: UAE releases footage showing air defenses intercepting and destroying drone
video:video.video:description: The UAE defense ministry said on Sunday a total of 117 drones were detected, with 113 intercepted and destroyed, while four fell within the country.

video:video.video:title: Selma commemorates Bloody Sunday as new voting rights concerns arise
video:video.video:description: Sixty-one years after state troopers attacked Civil Rights marchers on the Edmund Pettus Bridge in Selma, thousands gathered in the Alabama city on Sunday, amid new concerns about the future of the Voting Rights Act.

video:video.video:title: Bomb squad responds to a 'suspicious device' outside NYC mayor's house
video:video.video:description: Police officers responded on Sunday to a "suspicious device" which had been found in Manhattan's Upper East Side.

video:video.video:title: Iranians celebrate after Mojtaba Khamenei named supreme leader
video:video.video:description: Mojtaba Khamenei, the son of Iran's late supreme leader, has been named his successor, Iranian state TV announced early Monday, as the war that began with his father's killing less than two weeks ago took a dramatic turn.

video:video.video:title: Thousands march in countries across Latin America to mark International Women’s Day
video:video.video:description: Women across Latin America called for equal pay, reproductive rights, justice and celebrated women empowerment during events and demonstrations marking International Women’s Day on Sunday.

video:video.video:title: A new Nepali party led by an ex-rapper is set for a landslide win in parliamentary election
video:video.video:description: Preliminary and partial results show that a new political party led by an ex-rapper is leading Nepal’s parliamentary election, the country’s first since last year’s revolt.

video:video.video:title: Death toll rises in Lebanon as Israel escalates attacks causing widespread destruction
video:video.video:description: At least 10 people were killed in an Israeli strike on Sir al-Gharbiyeh in southern Lebanon on Sunday, according to Lebanon's Health Ministry.

video:video.video:title: 'Suspicious device' found in Manhattan, day after explosive device thrown at anti-Islam protest
video:video.video:description: Police in New York City on Sunday said that they were looking into a second suspicious device found in the same area of Manhattan's Upper East Side where a counterprotester during an anti-Islam demonstration threw an improvised explosive.

video:video.video:title: Bahrain’s king condemns 'unprecedented' and 'unjustifiable' Iranian attacks on Gulf States
video:video.video:description: King of Bahrain Hamad bin Isa Al Khalifa condemned the Iranian attacks on Gulf nations. Bahrain's authorities announced on Sunday that the country had intercepted and destroyed 3 missiles and 13 drones launched from Iran.

video:video.video:title: Smoke rises over Beirut after Lebanese capital rocked by blast as tension with Israel continues
video:video.video:description: Smoke rose into the sky above Beirut early Monday following an explosion in the Lebanese capital as tensions with Israel continued.

video:video.video:title: Tourists and staff airlifted out of Kenyan nature reserve amid deadly flooding across the country
video:video.video:description: Tourists and staff were airlifted from a national reserve in Kenya following severe flooding in the east African country.

video:video.video:title: Veteran lawmaker was shaped by conflict in the Middle East. Now he's grappling with Iran war
video:video.video:description: As Congress responds to President Trump’s attack on Iran, lawmakers who served in Iraq and Afghanistan find themselves front and center in a war debate that has taken on intensely personal meaning.

video:video.video:title: US military releases video said to show deadly strike on alleged drug boat in Eastern Pacific
video:video.video:description: The U.S. military said it killed six men Sunday in a strike on an alleged drug-smuggling vessel in the eastern Pacific Ocean as part of the Trump administration’s campaign against alleged traffickers.

video:video.video:title: Poland transforms from post-communist wreck to European growth champion in 35 years
video:video.video:description: Poland's economy has been transformed dramatically since the fall of communism in 1989. Once struggling, it is now one of Europe's most successful.

video:video.video:title: Vote count begins after Colombians cast ballot for Congress and presidential candidates
video:video.video:description: Colombians voted Sunday for a new Congress and to select candidates from three major coalitions in a primary-style contest ahead of a presidential election in May.
video:video.video:tag: Supreme Court of the United States

video:video.video:title: From needlepoint to mahjong, young people are embracing ‘grandma hobbies’
video:video.video:description: Droves of young people are turning to tactile, analog hobbies and activities, such as needlework and mahjong, as a means of escaping technology and reconnecting with creativity and exploration.

video:video.video:title: Supporters in Iran celebrate appointment of Mojtaba Khamenei as new supreme leader
video:video.video:description: Supporters took to streets of Tehran early on Monday to celebrate the appointment of Mojtaba Khamenei as Iran's new supreme leader.

video:video.video:title: Several killed, others missing after massive avalanche of garbage at Indonesia’s largest landfill
video:video.video:description: Rescuers are continuing to search for several people reported missing after a deadly avalanche of garbage at Indonesia’s largest landfill.

video:video.video:title: Qatar PM urges Iran to stop attacks 'right away'
video:video.video:description: Qatar’s prime minister said Iran had "betrayed" Doha by attacking Gulf countries, and urged all sides in the war to de-escalate the conflict.
